articles.title: Team SoterCare Selected as a Finalist in the NIA Innovation Voucher Programme 2026
articles.summary: Team SoterCare has been selected as a finalist in the National Innovation Agency (NIA) Innovation Voucher Programme 2026 under the University Voucher category. The team successfully presented its solution during the live pitch session and looks forward to the final results while continuing its mission to innovate in healthcare technology.
articles.body:
Team SoterCare is proud to announce its selection as a finalist in the National Innovation Agency (NIA) Innovation Voucher Programme 2026 under the University Voucher category.

As part of the final evaluation process, our team had the opportunity to present SoterCare during the live pitch session before an expert evaluation panel. The experience allowed us to showcase our vision, demonstrate our technology, receive valuable feedback from industry professionals, and represent our university on a national stage.

Reaching this milestone reflects the dedication, collaboration, and hard work of everyone involved in the project. We are sincerely grateful to our mentors, supporters, and everyone who has contributed to our journey.

We now look forward to the final results and remain committed to developing innovative technologies that create meaningful impact in healthcare and improve lives through accessible, intelligent solutions.

articles.title: Team SoterCare Named a Finalist at CodeSprint 11
articles.summary: Team SoterCare has been named one of the finalists advancing to the CodeSprint 11 Finale, the annual inter-university coding and innovation competition organised by the IEEE Student Branch.
articles.body:
CodeSprint 11 revealed its finalist lineup, and we're thrilled to share that SoterCare has earned its place among the 7 teams heading to the Finale. The competition has been a great platform to benchmark our solution, sharpen our thinking, and connect with some of the brightest student innovators in the country.

What makes this milestone meaningful is not just the selection itself, but the journey that led here. Every iteration of our solution, every piece of feedback we incorporated, every late night spent refining our approach, all of it contributed to where we stand today.

We are grateful to everyone who has supported and believed in us along the way.
More to come. Stay tuned.

articles.title: Acing Our CW2 Viva
articles.summary: Team SoterCare completed the final CW2 viva with Mr. Banu Athuraliya and landed among the highest marks. We also walked away with some great challenges to push our project further.
articles.body:
After months of building, testing, and refining SoterCare, the sensors, the dashboards, the gait model, the pitches, the project finally faced its toughest challenge yet, the CW2 viva. This wasn't a pitch to investors or a panel of competition judges, it was a direct examination of the work itself in front of someone who knows exactly where the gaps are likely to be.

We sat down with Mr. Banu Athuraliya for our final CW2 viva and came out with the highest marks on the SDGP module. More importantly the session didn't just confirm what's working, it surfaced some genuinely great challenges that we now need to think through as we push SoterCare further. 

Every milestone in this project so far has worked the same way, progress shows up as much in the feedback as it does in the win. 
Challenge accepted. Team SoterCare is ready to ace the next phase!

articles.title: Team Completes The First Data Recording Session for Risky Movement Detection
articles.summary: Team SoterCare successfully wrapped up its first structured data recording session for the "Risky Movement Detection" model, with fellow IITians volunteering to wear our thigh band and walk us through real, everyday movement. The session is a key milestone in building a dataset robust enough to recognize pre-fall behavior before it ever becomes a fall.
articles.body:
We put out a call for volunteers to help us build something we believe elderly care has been missing, a way to act before a fall happens, not just react after one. Most alarm systems only ring once someone has already hit the ground. Our goal with SoterCare has always been prevention and prevention starts with data.

That call became a full recording session. Using SoterCare Studio, our custom built data capture tool, volunteers were fitted with thigh-worn IMU sensors recording at 50Hz while they moved through a guided sequence of everyday actions. Each session was logged, labelled, and reviewed in real time so we could catch noisy or incomplete recordings before they ever reached the dataset.

Volunteers were guided through five core movement states that make up the backbone of our gait model: walking, standing idle, sitting idle, standing up, and sitting down. What stood out most was how willing our fellow students were to give up their time for this. Every recording, every repeated "stand up, sit down, walk a few steps and back" was a small but real contribution to a dataset that will eventually train a model meant to protect someone's grandparent. We're genuinely grateful for that.

This dataset now feeds directly into our Proactive Gait Analysis Model, the on-device classifier that pairs a risky transition prediction with a silent haptic warning. The more varied and well labelled our data, the sharper that model gets at telling a normal wobble apart from a genuinely risky one.

We're now moving into the next phase cleaning, labelling, and feeding this new batch of recordings into training. We'll be sharing results as the model improves. As always, this milestone belongs to everyone who showed up, sensor strapped to their thigh, and walked back and forth across a room for data collection.

With thanks to every volunteer who contributed.

articles.title: Team SoterCare's Pitching Journey
articles.summary: Team SoterCare advanced through the Hult Prize IIT Qualifier Round, was named one of twelve finalists for the Hult Prize On-Campus Finale 2026 and went on to compete at the Grand Finale.
articles.body:
The journey began with the Qualifier Round, where we got the chance to present our idea, showcase our vision, and compete alongside inspiring teams. Following the qualifiers, SoterCare had officially qualified for the Hult Prize IIT Finals, one of 12 finalists selected from the on-campus competition heading into the Grand Finale.

The experience gave us valuable feedback and clear direction to improve. Every question from the judges, every moment on that stage, added something to how we think about SoterCare and where we need to take it next. 

A heartfelt thank you to everyone who supported us and believed in our idea, your motivation meant a lot to us along the way. This is only the beginning, we are moving on to the next challenge, stronger and better!

articles.title: U.S. Delegation Visit from the University of Oklahoma
articles.summary: Team SoterCare had the privilege of delivering an elevator pitch to a visiting delegation from the University of Oklahoma, an experience that opened the door to global perspectives on innovation, entrepreneurship, and meaningful impact.
articles.body:
We had the privilege of participating in the U.S. Delegation Visit from the University of Oklahoma, where we presented an elevator pitch to Heather Johnston and engaged in a meaningful discussion followed by valuable feedback.

As part of the showcase, our team presented SoterCare directly to the visiting delegation, outlining the problem we are solving in healthcare accessibility, the technology behind our solution, and the impact we hope to create. The session was followed by a focused discussion in which the delegation shared feedback drawn from their experience with entrepreneurship programmes, perspective that was both practical and encouraging.

For us, the most valuable part of the visit was not the pitch itself but the conversation that followed. Hearing how practitioners and educators at the University of Oklahoma think about early stage ventures gave us a clearer lens through which to reflect on our own assumptions, our go-to-market thinking, and what it truly means to build something that scales with purpose.

This experience provided global exposure, practical insights, and a deeper understanding of innovation and impact. We left the day with renewed clarity, stronger connections, and a deeper appreciation for what global exposure can do for a student team still in the process of shaping its identity. Moments like these remind us why we started.
